First Finding: critical/security: SQL injection vulnerability: user_id is concatenated directly into the query without parameterization

Description: Hardcoded API secret in source code
First Finding: critical/security: Hardcoded Stripe secret key exposed in source code. This key must be loaded from environment variables or secure configuration; never committed to repo.
critical/security: dangerouslySetInnerHTML with unsanitized 'body' enables XSS attacks. Sanitize HTML before rendering or use safe text rendering.

Description: Missing auth check on admin endpoint
First Finding: critical/security: Missing authentication/authorization check. Any user can delete any user without permission verification.
critical/security: Storing plaintext password instead of hash. This removes security and exposes user credentials if database is compromised.
critical/security: Using eval() is a critical security vulnerability. It executes arbitrary code and enables code injection attacks.
critical/security: Path traversal vulnerability: filename from req.query.file is not validated; allowing access to arbitrary files via '../' sequences
critical/security: SECRET_KEY appears to be hardcoded or unvalidated. Ensure it's from secure environment variable and not exposed.

Description: CORS allowing all origins with credentials
First Finding: critical/security: Setting origin: '*' with credentials: true violates CORS security. Browsers reject this combination. Use specific origins instead.
critical/security: yaml.load() is unsafe; use yaml.safe_load() to prevent arbitrary code execution attacks.
critical/security: Logging password in plaintext is a critical security risk. Passwords should never be logged.
critical/security: MD5 is cryptographically broken. Use hashlib.sha256() instead for secure token hashing.
critical/security: os.system() with unsanitized f-string is vulnerable to shell injection if branch contains special characters or user input.
critical/security: Unvalidated user input passed to requests.get() enables Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) attacks. Validate and restrict URLs before use.

Description: Exposing stack trace in error response
First Finding: major/security: Exposing error.stack to clients reveals sensitive information like file paths and internal code structure. Only return stack in development mode.
major/bug: user may be null if findById returns no result; causing null dereference on user.name

Description: Infinite loop with no exit condition
First Finding: major/bug: Infinite loop with no break condition or exception handling. Worker will hang if queue.get() blocks and cannot process shutdown signals.

Description: Deleting all records instead of filtered
First Finding: critical/bug: Removing the expiration filter causes delete_many({}) to delete ALL sessions instead of only expired ones; causing data loss.
major/bug: Changed >= to > breaks existing behavior. Orders with quantity exactly equal to min_quantity no longer get discounted.

Description: Writing to wrong variable — result lost
First Finding: major/bug: Discount applied to subtotal AFTER tax calculation. Tax should be recalculated on discounted amount.
critical/bug: Arguments to ledger.record() are swapped: to_account and from_account are reversed; causing transfers to record in opposite direction.

Description: Division by zero on empty list
First Finding: major/bug: Division by zero when scores is empty. len(scores) could be 0; causing ZeroDivisionError.
major/bug: File handle 'f' is never closed; will leak file descriptor. Use context manager or explicit close().
critical/bug: Bare except clause silently swallows all exceptions including payment failures. Failures must be logged and returned to the caller.
critical/bug: Assignment operator (=) used instead of comparison (==). This silently mutates user.role and always evaluates to truthy.
major/bug: Mutable default argument `items=[]` is shared across calls; causing unexpected state mutation
major/bug: Missing return statement or error handling. Function doesn't indicate success/failure of decrement operation.
major/bug: conn.Close() called twice: once in defer (line 21) and again explicitly (line 23). This causes double-close.
critical/bug: Formula multiplies by 365 unconditionally. This calculates annual cost; not per-transaction cost. Clarify intended behavior and whether 365 should be conditional.
major/bug: mailer.send() is called without await. If async; the email may not send before logger.info() executes.
critical/bug: Logic error: condition uses 'or' instead of 'and'. Will always be true for any age value.
major/bug: Missing return statement after error response. Code will continue executing and call createUser with incomplete body.

Description: String compared to number without conversion

First Finding: major/bug: Removing items from a list while iterating over it causes elements to be skipped. Use a list comprehension or filter instead.

Description: setTimeout in critical path causes race
First Finding: major/risky_change: setTimeout with 0ms delay defers inventory update; creating race condition where order returns success before inventory is actually updated.
major/bug: EMAIL_REGEX allows consecutive '@' symbols and empty local/domain parts due to greedy '*' quantifiers. Use '+' or add anchors.
major/bug: Floating-point equality check (final == 29.99) is unreliable due to precision errors. Use approximate comparison instead.
major/bug: Assignments overwrite result instead of appending. Loop will only retain the last transformed item.
major/risky_change: Removing error wrapping loses the underlying error context. This breaks debugging and error chains. Use %w to preserve the original error.
major/risky_change: Goroutine launched without lifecycle management. No way to stop it; will run indefinitely and leak on shutdown.
major/bug: setInterval is not cleaned up in useEffect. This causes a memory leak and multiple intervals to accumulate on re-renders.
major/bug: Comparing user.role to boolean true is likely a type error. If role is a string like 'admin'; this will always be false. Use proper role check.
major/bug: Missing null/key check: prices dict may not contain 'usd' key; causing KeyError at runtime
major/bug: Switch statement has no default case and doesn't return a value for unhandled status values; causing undefined return.
major/bug: Hardcoded loop limit (7) with no retry logic. If all requests fail; exceptions propagate with no backoff or retry mechanism.
major/bug: JSON.parse will throw on empty string. Parsing '' always fails and crashes the try block.
major/bug: Email validation only checks for '@' presence. This allows invalid emails like 'a@' or '@b'. Use a proper regex or library.
major/security: URL constructed via string concatenation allows injection attacks. Use URL parameters or proper encoding.
nit/style: Use 'if feature.active:' instead of '== True' for clearer boolean comparison.
major/bug: Accessing items[0].name without checking if items array is empty or exists. Will throw runtime error if items is empty.
major/security: Logging decoded JWT token exposes sensitive user data (claims; user IDs; roles). Remove before shipping.
minor/style: React is already imported from 'react' on line 1; importing hooks from same module creates redundant imports
minor/style: Inconsistent error message formatting: 'items' error uses '!!!' while 'customer_id' uses plain text. Standardize for clarity.

Description: Sync file I/O in async handler
First Finding: major/bug: readFileSync is synchronous and blocks the event loop. Use fs.promises.readFile or fs.readFile with callback for async handling.

Description: Generic error message hides root cause
First Finding: major/bug: Catching IntegrityError silently hides duplicate key or constraint violations. Should re-raise or handle specifically.
major/security: String comparison for role check is case-sensitive and fragile. Use enum or normalized constant to prevent auth bypass via capitalization variations.
major/performance: new Date() and toLocaleDateString() called on every render; creates unnecessary object garbage and slow locale parsing repeated needlessly.
major/performance: N+1 query: fetching Customer for each order individually. Use join or bulk load instead.
major/security: Missing salt parameter in pbkdf2Sync. Current code passes undefined salt; weakening the hash security.

Description: String concat in loop instead of join
First Finding: major/performance: String concatenation in loop is O(n²). Use list and ''.join() instead for better performance.
major/risky_change: status column uses TEXT without constraints. Use ENUM or CHECK for valid values to prevent invalid statuses.

Description: SELECT * when only one column needed
First Finding: major/bug: SQL query is vulnerable to injection. Use parameterized queries instead of string formatting.
major/bug: Regex raw string uses single backslashes instead of double. Will cause SyntaxError: bad escape sequence.

Description: Expensive Intl object created each render
O(n^2) nested loop instead of Set
First Finding: major/bug: Nested loop finds all matches without deduplication. If 'a' matches multiple elements in list_b; it gets appended multiple times.
major/risky_change: Rate limiting middleware removed from apiRouter. This removes protection against abuse and DoS attacks on the /data endpoint.
major/risky_change: Response format changed from array to object. This silently breaks all clients expecting a JSON array; risking widespread integration failures.
major/risky_change: Removed schema validation check. This removes the guard against invalid input; allowing unvalidated data to reach process().
critical/bug: Converting amount to INTEGER loses decimal precision. Financial amounts must use NUMERIC or DECIMAL to prevent data corruption.
major/risky_change: Removing ErrorBoundary wrapper removes runtime error handling. Unhandled React errors will now crash the entire app instead of showing ErrorPage.
critical/risky_change: Removed db.transaction() wrapper. Debit and credit are now non-atomic; if credit fails; money is lost. Restores the guard.
critical/security: verify=False disables SSL certificate verification. This allows MITM attacks. Remove this or use proper certificate validation.
major/risky_change: Removal of retry logic silently changes behavior. Payment failures that were previously retried (up to 3 attempts) will now fail immediately.

Description: Proper error handling with specific exception
First Finding: major/bug: Catching only FileNotFoundError misses other I/O issues (permission denied; invalid JSON). Consider catching broader exceptions or let them propagate.

Description: Using enum instead of string literals
Using context manager for DB session
First Finding: major/bug: user can be None if user_id doesn't exist; accessing user.name will raise AttributeError
major/bug: Password validation uses data.get('password'; '') which returns empty string if key missing. len('') < 8 is True; so validation always fails when password is absent.

Description: Using defer for resource cleanup in Go
